title: CD Media for High Density Cultivation of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ca7g7","text":"CellDeg's High Density Cultivation system allows for efficient accumulation of biomass of photosynthetic organisms, such as cyanobacteria and microalgae. The media requires a higher nutrient concentration, to facilitate high growth rates over extended periods. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f6mg0","text":"This protocol describes the media composition used in the Lindberg lab at Ångström laboratory (Uppsala University). The basic composition was previously described in Lippi et al., [1], and has been slightly modified:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"202tl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"29knr","text":"different iron source used","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"97eb","text":"NaNO3 as sole (major) nitrogen source (no additional KNO3)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":4,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":56,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"865ip","text":"no cobalt is added to the standard media ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9km26","text":"no copper is added to the standard media","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1mud8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e6pqt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"53vdd","text":"[1] Lippi L, Bähr L, Wüstenberg A, Wilde A, Steuer R: Exploring the potential of high-density cultivation of cyanobacteria for the production of cyanophycin. Algal Research 2018, 31:363-366","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the following 8 stock solutions %s %s Filter sterilize all solutions and store them at 4-10 °C (fridge) If you are working with a promoter that is induced by metals (e.g. Zn 2+ , Cu 2+ , Co 2+ ), you can omit that salt from the Trace elements mix and supply it from a sterile filtered stock solution on demand. Synechocystis 6803 can grow to high densities without any Cu 2+ and Co 2+ in the CD medium!

Preparation of 1 L CD medium fill ~500 mL H 2 O into an autoclavable 1 L bottle → do not mix the stock solution without excess H 2 O to prevent salt precipitation add the following stock solutions before sterilization Stock A - 1 mL Stock B - 1 mL Stock C - 1 mL Stock E - 10 mL EDTA - 1 mL Trace elements - 1 mL add ddH 2 O to a final volume of 1 L autoclave according to your standard procedure autoclaving leads to a significant loss of media volume! ⇒ Fill up the media with autoclaved ddH 2 O (work under the clean bench) the white precipitate will disappear upon addition of Stock F (Step 3)

Addition of stocks after autoclaving Stock D - 1 mL Stock F - 4 mL → work under sterile conditions (clean bench) Stock D contains Fe(III), which tends to precipitate during autoclaving Stock F contains HCO 3 - , which vaporizes under heat treatment Media can be stored at room temperature
creator: Oliver Mantovani
